com.hp.workpath.api.printer

Class PrinterService

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hp.workpath.api.printer.PrinterService

  • public final class PrinterService
extends java.lang.Object

    PrinterService provides an easy to use interface to initiate printing related built-in tasks. In its simplest form, the printing task can be initiated by simply calling submit(Context, PrintAttributes, PrintletAttributes). For more control of attributes or to present its own UI, the PrintletAttributes must be built prior to calling submit(Context, PrintAttributes, PrintletAttributes).

    A call to submit(Context, PrintAttributes, PrintletAttributes) starts the printing task. The printing process is long running and asynchronous by nature, and details of its progress are communicated to the calling entity via a AbstractJobletObserver. The application must create an object of AbstractJobletObserver and register/unregister it based on the component's life cycle, i.e. Activity or Service.

    Depending upon the task (see SupportedTask) being submitted, its completion is communicated via a call to ILetObserver.onComplete(String, JobInfo)

    Selecting Attributes

    For finer control of the attributes passed to submit(Context, PrintAttributes, PrintletAttributes), note that the application must determine the existing capability of the device. These capabilities differ per devices. A call to getCapabilities(Context, Result) Retrieves these capabilities to the caller.

    Submitting a Job

    The following is an excerpt showing how to select attributes and submit a print job: 

     private static final class PrintAsyncTask extends AsyncTask<Void, Void, Void> {
 
    
 protected Void doInBackground(final Void... params) {
 Result result = new Result();
 final PrintAttributesCaps caps = PrinterService.getCapabilities(
 mContext, result);
 
    
 if (null == caps) {
 // Unable to retrieve capabilities. Need to look at the result to
 // see what happened.
 return null;
 }
 
    
 try {
 final Duplex duplex =
 Duplex.valueOf(mPrefs.getString(PrintConfigureFragment.PREF_DUPLEX_MODE, Duplex.DEFAULT.name()));
 
    
 .....
 
    
 // Create the print attributes
 final PrintAttributes attributes =
 new PrintAttributes.PrintFromStorageBuilder(Uri.fromFile(new File(filePath)))
 .setColorMode(cm)
 .setDuplex(duplex)
 .setAutoFit(af)
 .setStapleMode(sm)
 .setPaperSource(psrc)
 .setPaperSize(psz)
 .setDocumentFormat(dfmt)
 .setCopies(copies)
 .build(caps);
 
    
 // Create the printlet attributes
 final PrintletAttributes taskAttribs = new PrintletAttributes.Builder().build();
 
    
 // Submit the job
 PrinterService.submit(mContext, attributes, taskAttribs);
 
    
 } catch (final CapabilitiesExceededException cee) {
 // Either an attribute was outside of the capabilities or the
 // capabilities were empty.
 }
 }
 }

    Supported Tasks

    • Print from local Storage
    • Print from HTTP
    • Print from Stream
    • Print from Usb

    Print from local Storage

    When executed on the device, this task prints a local file using the given attributes. A local file is one which resides on the local file system of the printer.

    Print from HTTP

    When executed on the device, this task prints a remote file using the given http url.

    Print from Stream

    When executed on the device, this task prints from the given streams.

    Print from Usb

    When executed on the device, this task prints a file from the given Usb url.

    Monitoring the Job

    After submitting the job, it can be monitored by using the monitorJobInForeground(Activity, int, JobletAttributes, Intent) API and providing the job Id.

    Canceling the Job

    To cancel a submitted job, you can use cancelJob(Context, int)
    Since:
    API 1

    • Method Detail

      • submit

        public static java.lang.String submit(android.content.Context context,
                                      PrintAttributes printAttributes,
                                      PrintletAttributes letAttributes)

        Submits a print job asynchronously to the Printer using the provided print attributes. If AbstractJobletObserver is present, the AbstractJobletObserver will receive call backs indicating job progress, completion and errors.

        This task can accept multiple requests.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context object for your activity or application.
        printAttributes - Attributes related to print.

        PrintAttributes default values: The default value can be figured out by getDefaults(Context, Result)

        • ColorMode = If printer is mono model, the default value is PrintAttributes.ColorMode.MONO. If printer is color model, the default value is PrintAttributes.ColorMode.COLOR.
        • Duplex = This default value can be changed in EWS > Copy/Print > Default Print Options > Output Sides menu.
        • AutoFit = PrintAttributes.AutoFit.TRUE
        • Copies = 1. This default value can be changed in EWS > Copy/Print > Default Print Options > Number of Copies menu.
        • FileUri = Empty
        • StapleMode = This default value can be changed in EWS > Copy/Print > Default Print Options > Staple menu. This menu is shown in EWS when finisher to support Staple feature is installed.
        • PaperSource = PrintAttributes.PaperSource.AUTO
        • PaperSize = This default value can be changed in EWS > Copy/Print > Default Print Options > Default Paper Size menu.
        • PaperType = This default value can be changed in EWS > Copy/Print > Default Print Options > Paper Type menu.
        • DocumentFormat = PrintAttributes.DocumentFormat.PDF
        • CollateMode = PrintAttributes.CollateMode.UNCOLLATED
        • JobName = null
        • Orientation = PrintAttributes.Orientation.PORTRAIT
        • PrintQuality = This default value can be changed in EWS > Copy/Print > Default Print Options > Quality and Speed menu.
        • OutputBin = This default value can be changed in EWS > Copy/Print > Default Print Options > Output Bin menu. This menu is shown in EWS when finisher is installed. This default value is different according to printing engine and finisher installation.
        • StartPageRanges = 0
        • EndPageRanges = 0
        • Finishings = This default value can be changed in EWS > Copy/Print > Default Print Options > Hole Punch, Staple, Fold menu. Hole Punch, Staple and Fold menus are shown in EWS when finisher to support Hole Punch, Staple and Fold feature is installed.

        letAttributes - Attributes related to how the PrinterService should behave.
        Returns:

        a unique request ID to track this request

        • Return value shouldn't be empty or null.

        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if context or PrintAttributes are null with disabled Show Setting UI
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if incompatible attributes are provided (e.g. PrintAttributes has Settings UI false and PrintAttributes are null)
        Since:
        API 1

      • submit

        public static java.lang.String submit(android.content.Context context,
                                      java.util.ArrayList<PrintAttributes> printAttributes,
                                      PrintletAttributes letAttributes)

        Submits a print job asynchronously to the Printer using the provided print attributes. If AbstractJobletObserver is present, the AbstractJobletObserver will receive call backs indicating job progress, completion and errors.

        This task can accept multiple requests.

        Note: When more than one batch is submitted (e.g., batch1, batch2, batch3), the batches will be queued and processed in FIFO (First-In-First-Out) order. This means that batch1 will be processed first, followed by batch2, and then batch3. The next batch will start processing only after the previous batch has completed.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context object for your activity or application.
        printAttributes - Attributes related to print.
        letAttributes - Attributes related to how the PrinterService should behave.
        Returns:

        a unique request ID to track this request

        • Return value shouldn't be empty or null.

        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if context or PrintAttributes are null with disabled Show Setting UI
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if incompatible attributes are provided (e.g. PrintAttributes has Settings UI false and PrintAttributes are null)
        Since:
        API 7

      • getCapabilities

        public static PrintAttributesCaps getCapabilities(android.content.Context context,
                                                  Result result)

        This synchronous operation returns the intersection of Printer and PrinterService capabilities. In this example, for the color mode attribute:

        • The color modes supported from the Printer are {mono, auto}
        • The color modes of the PrinterService are {mono, color}

            • Therefore, the color mode capabilities will include only {mono}.

            Note that for some attributes, the intersection of values may result in empty capabilities.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context object for your activity or application.
        result - (optional) Indicates any errors which occurred while retrieving capabilities.
        Returns:
        Capabilities found both on the Printer and in the PrinterService or null.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if context is null
        Since:
        API 1

      • getDefaults

        public static PrintAttributes getDefaults(android.content.Context context,
                                          Result result)
        Retrieves the device default values for print attributes. If the value is DEFAULT, it means it is not supported attribute from the device.
        Parameters:
        context - The Context object for your activity or application.
        result - (optional) Indicates any errors which occurred while retrieving defaults.
        Returns:
        PrinterAttributes containing default values for submitting a job
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if context is null
        Since:
        API 1

      • isSupported

        public static boolean isSupported(android.content.Context context)

        This method is needed to determine if the service is supported or not. If it's not supported, PrinterService operation will be failed.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context in which the application is running.
        Returns:

        boolean Returns result of supported.

        • If service is supported, method returns true.
        • If service is not supported, method returns false.

        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Returns error if context is null.
        Since:
        API 1
